intro: Conanthus origanifolius, also known as the 'Origanum-type' or 'Thyme-leaved Sage', is a flowering plant species within the Lamiaceae family. The leaves of Conanthus origanifolius have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Conanthus origanifolius is often utilized in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to support digestive health, alleviate respiratory issues, and combat infections.
